steps.title: Raise the silver percentage
steps.content: Coins below 80% silver were considered unsuitable for trade, and the 367 BCE coin had only 74.2% silver. If the amount of silver stayed the same while the coin's total weight decreased, silver would make up a larger percentage of the coin, helping restore confidence.

steps.title: Reject ineffective fixes
steps.content: Choice B only changes a stated threshold without changing the coins themselves. Choice C adds copper, worsening the silver proportion. Choice D keeps the same below-threshold silver-to-copper proportion while making coins heavier, so traders would still see unsuitable currency. The answer is A.
